title: Introduction to Molecular and Nanoscale Principles
catalogDescription: Introduction to theories and concepts of molecular and nanoscale systems to raise awareness of technological and societal transformations anticipated through progress in nanotechnology. Prerequisite: either CHEM 142, CHEM 144, or CHEM 145; either MATH 126 or MATH 136; PHYS 121.

title: Nanoscience and Molecular Engineering Seminar I
catalogDescription: Introduces nanoscience and molecular engineering, and offers the opportunity of a first intellectual and interdisciplinary forum with interactions with members of research groups in the field. First in a series of three. Prerequisite: NME 220, which may be taken concurrently. Credit/no-credit only.

title: Nanoscience and Molecular Engineering Seminar II
catalogDescription: Demonstrates how to tackle research challenges, put results into the framework of the open literature, and present and interpret data. Discusses contemporary societal and ethical aspects in nanoscience and molecular engineering. Second in a series of three. Credit/no-credit only.

title: Nanoscience and Molecular Engineering Seminar III
catalogDescription: Provides students with the opportunity to present their research results obtained during undergraduate research studies. Assessing the work of their peers and receiving feedback on their work, students further refine their ability in conducting and presenting research. Third in a series of three. Prerequisite: NME 321. Credit/no-credit only.
